meta.title: Initiating Devices and Control Panels — Technical Reference
meta.description: Manual call points, interface modules, relay modules, and control panel specifications

manual_initiating_devices.description: One pull motion to activate — no cover or latch

mounting_height.nfpa72: 3.5 ft to 4.5 ft (1.07m to 1.37m) — to operable part
mounting_height.notes: NFPA and BS heights differ — use local standard

manual_initiating_devices.contacts: Normally open (NO) dry contact — closes on activation
manual_initiating_devices.notes: Most common type in North America

manual_initiating_devices.description: Requires two actions to activate (lift cover + pull lever) — prevents accidental activation
manual_initiating_devices.activation: Lift protective cover, then pull handle downward
manual_initiating_devices.use: Areas where accidental activation is likely (schools, public areas, stadiums)
manual_initiating_devices.notes: Required in some jurisdictions for specific occupancies

manual_initiating_devices.description: Smash glass element to activate — traditional European design
manual_initiating_devices.notes: Being replaced by resettable (plastic) elements in modern installations. EN 54-11 now prefers resettable elements.
manual_initiating_devices.status: LEGACY — being phased out in favor of resettable type

manual_initiating_devices.description: Push or poke element that resets without replacement — modern European standard
manual_initiating_devices.reset_method: Special key tool resets element (no parts to replace)
manual_initiating_devices.notes: Preferred modern type — lower maintenance cost, no consumable parts

interface_modules.description: Monitors a normally-open (NO) or normally-closed (NC) dry contact device

connections.input: 2 wires to dry contact device + 2 wires to SLC (addressable) or IDC (conventional)
connections.type: NO or NC (configurable, usually DIP switch)

interface_modules.description: Provides a relay output to control an external device

relay_specs.contacts: Normally open (NO) + Normally closed (NC) — form C
relay_specs.voltage: Isolated relay — does not share common with system

interface_modules.activation: Programmed via panel software (on alarm, supervisory, or fault)
interface_modules.notes: Critical for system integration — ensure relay ratings match controlled device

interface_modules.description: Combines monitor input and control relay in one module
Isolates a section of the SLC when a short-circuit is detected, allowing the rest of the loop to function
interface_modules.automatic_reset: Auto-resets when short circuit is removed
interface_modules.placement: Between each floor or zone on the SLC loop, or every 20 devices
interface_modules.notes: Essential for reliability in addressable systems. Some detectors have built-in isolators.

interface_modules.description: Provides multiple relay outputs from the control panel
interface_modules.use_for: Large buildings with many integration points

interface_modules.description: Used during commissioning to identify devices on the SLC loop
interface_modules.function: When plugged into a device, causes the panel to identify that specific address
interface_modules.notes: Commissioning tool only — not permanent installation

control_panels.description: Zoned panel — identifies alarm zone but not individual device
control_panels.zone_devices: Maximum 20 detectors per zone (recommended for reliable supervision)
control_panels.device_types: 2-wire or 4-wire smoke detectors, heat detectors, pull stations, bells/horns
control_panels.identification: Zone-level only (LED per zone or LCD with zone number)
cons: Cannot pinpoint exact device in alarm
control_panels.typical_use: Small buildings, simple layouts, schools, small offices, residential

control_panels.description: Communicates individually with every device on the SLC loop — identifies exact device in alarm
control_panels.devices_per_loop: Typically 100 to 250 devices per loop (manufacturer dependent)
control_panels.device_identification: Individual device address — panel shows exact device type, location label, and status

protocols.notes: Panels only work with same-brand devices

features: Event logging (hundreds to thousands of events)
control_panels.typical_use: Medium to large buildings, hospitals, high-rise, industrial, shopping malls

control_panels.description: Addressable panel where devices send actual sensor values (smoke level, temperature) — not just alarm/fault states
control_panels.difference_from_addressable: Standard addressable = device decides (on/off). Intelligent = panel decides based on algorithms and sensor data.
features: Multi-criteria analysis (panel processes smoke + heat + CO data together)
Adaptive algorithms that learn normal environment

control_panels.description: Multiple fire alarm panels networked together to form a unified system across a campus or large building

network_types.examples: Notifier NOTI-FIRE-NET, Simplex 4100ES Network, Edwards EST Network

network_types.description: Uses standard Ethernet networking — modern approach
Long-distance network backbone for campus installations

control_panels.notes: Master panel (fire command center) can monitor and control all sub-panels in the network

control_panels.description: Fire alarm panel with integrated voice evacuation amplifier and zone paging
control_panels.notes: Often a separate panel that works in conjunction with the detection panel

control_panels.description: Panel with built-in wireless receiver for communication with wireless devices
control_panels.signal_supervision: Continuous monitoring of wireless signal strength
control_panels.battery_monitoring: Each wireless device battery level reported to panel
control_panels.battery_life: 3 to 5 years typical for wireless detectors
control_panels.notes: Must register with local radio frequency authority in some countries

ancillary_equipment.description: Remote display panel showing system status, zoned floor plan, and device status

display_types.description: LED matrix with zone labels — traditional
Color LCD showing building floor plan with device status
Software running on dedicated PC with large monitor(s)

ancillary_equipment.location: Building entrance, fire command center, security desk, building management office
ancillary_equipment.requirements: Must show alarm, trouble, and supervisory status for all zones

ancillary_equipment.type: Graphic Annunciator with Building Floor Plan
ancillary_equipment.description: Custom engraved or printed building floor plan with LED indicators at each device/zone location
ancillary_equipment.use: Large facilities — provides instant visual identification of alarm location
ancillary_equipment.notes: Often required for healthcare, high-rise, and campus facilities

ancillary_equipment.description: Holds fire-rated door open using electromagnetic force — releases on power removal
ancillary_equipment.release: Power removal (fail-safe) OR fire alarm signal
ancillary_equipment.notes: Critical device — must be supervised by fire alarm panel

ancillary_equipment.description: Termination resistor for conventional circuits (Class B) — enables wire supervision

typical_values.note: ALWAYS use manufacturer-specified value — wrong value causes false troubles

ancillary_equipment.location: At the last device on the circuit (not in the panel)

ancillary_equipment.description: Mounting housing for duct smoke detector with sampling tube
ancillary_equipment.installation: Mounted on side of duct with sampling tube extending into air stream
ancillary_equipment.tube_length: Must cross at least 75% of duct width
ancillary_equipment.access_panel: Removable cover for detector access and maintenance

ancillary_equipment.description: EMT, rigid, or flexible conduit for fire alarm wiring protection
ancillary_equipment.notes: Conduit requirements vary by code and installation type

nomenclature_map.description: Common abbreviations used in fire alarm systems

terms.FACU: Fire Alarm Control Unit (same as FACP)
terms.CIE: Control and Indicating Equipment (EN 54 term for FACP)
